section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports experiencing left hand pain and a grade two out of six systolic ejection murmur was appreciated. He also reports his lungs are clear without wheezes, rales or rhonchi and his abdomen is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The murmur, lung, and abdominal descriptions are exam findings, not patient-reported symptoms. Placing them in HPI blurs the distinction between subjective history and objective exam.

section_miscategorizations.text: Physical exam revealed a grade two out of six systolic ejection murmur was appreciated on lung auscultation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The content belongs in Findings, but the phrase 'on lung auscultation' is anatomically inaccurate for a cardiac murmur and could confuse interpretation of the exam location, though it does not change the appropriate section.
